programme.title: Hudson & Rex
programme.desc: When a local whale watching boat mysteriously returns to harbor without its passengers, Charlie and Rex must race against time to find the missing tourists.

programme.title: Hudson & Rex
programme.desc: As a pair of armed robbers rip through St. John's, Charlie and Rex work fast to stop them by uncovering the truth behind the spree.

programme.title: Hudson & Rex
programme.desc: When a prominent Judge disappears, Charlie and Rex piece together the crime with the help of a blind true crime podcaster who insists she heard a murder but could not see the culprit.

programme.title: Hudson & Rex
programme.desc: In a case that unfolds in real-time, Charlie and Rex have one hour to disarm a masked gunman holding the employees of an insurance office hostage.

programme.title: Hudson & Rex
programme.desc: A murder in a local park points to someone dressed as The Night Keeper.

programme.title: Our Idiot Brother
programme.desc: Ned means well, but has no common sense. When he barges into the lives of his three sisters, hilarity ensues.

programme.title: Big Wedding, The
programme.desc: A long-divorced couple fakes being married as their family unites for a wedding.

programme.title: I Feel Pretty
programme.desc: A woman struggling with insecurity wakes from a fall believing she is the most beautiful woman on the planet.

programme.title: Long Shot
programme.desc: A journalist reunites with his childhood crush, now one of the most influential women in the world.

programme.title: Chef [Broadcast Edit]
programme.desc: After a controlling owner (Dustin Hoffman) pushes him too far, chef Carl Casper (Jon Favreau) quits his position at a prestigious Los Angeles restaurant. As he tries to figure out what his next step should be, he finds himself in Miami. Carl joins forces with his ex-wife (Sofía Vergara), best friend (John Leguizamo) and son (Emjay Anthony) to launch a food-truck business, and the venture provides a chance to reignite his passion for cooking -- as well as his zest for life and love.

programme.title: Direct Contact
programme.desc: An American prisoner in a Russian jail is given a chance at freedom if he can rescue a kidnapped woman.

programme.title: Homeboy [Broadcast Edit]
programme.desc: Though only in his 20s, professional boxer Johnny Walker is on the downswing of his career and suffering from a mild form of brain damage. Resettling in the beachfront resort of Asbury Park, N.J., he falls in with shady promoter Wesley Pendergrass and meets kindhearted boardwalk operator Ruby. When Wesley proposes cutting Johnny in on a criminal undertaking, Johnny must choose between a life of crime and a settled future with Ruby.

programme.title: Killing Season
programme.desc: Oscar winner Robert De Niro ("Raging Bull") and Oscar nominee John Travolta ("Pulp Fiction") star as two Bosnian War veterans hunting each other in this thrilling game of cat and mouse.

programme.title: Dead Man
programme.desc: On the run after murdering a man, accountant William Blake encounters a strange Native American man named Nobody who prepares him for his journey into the spiritual world.

programme.title: Jobs
programme.desc: The story of Steve Jobs' ascension from college dropout into one of the most revered creative entrepreneurs of the 20th century

programme.title: Security
programme.desc: A security guard protects an eleven year-old girl who is being targeted by a gang for participating

programme.title: Legionnaire
programme.desc: Alain is a boxer in 1925 Marseille, France. When he doesn't take the dive paid to take by a mob boss, he has to split.

programme.title: The Take
programme.desc: After he's shot during a heist in East L.A., an armored-truck driver (Leguizamo) wrestles with rehabilitation and tracking down the man (Gibson) who committed the crime.

programme.title: Anacondas: The Hunt For The Blood Orchid
programme.desc: A team of scientists braves the dangers of the rain forest in search of a flower that contains a youth serum.

programme.title: The Lazarus Project
programme.desc: A harrowing and frightening thriller about a man who has everything he's ever loved stripped away from him; and to earn his life and family back, he must face obstacles of mystical origins, endure countless tests of his faith, struggle with his own sanity, and explore the depth and the power of his soul.

programme.title: The Fog
programme.desc: One hundred years after their ship was deliberately wrecked, the ghosts of the murdered seaman have returned to Antonio Bay to exact a ghastly vengeance.

programme.title: Eight Iron Men
programme.desc: A beleaguered Army squadron risk their lives to rescue a fellow soldier trapped behind enemy lines.

programme.title: Pushover
programme.desc: An honest detective's pursuit of a bank robber drags him into a deadly affair with the crook's moll.

programme.title: A Night To Remember
programme.desc: Mystery writer Jeff Troy (Brian Aherne) and his lovely wife Nancy (Loretta Young) move into a Greenwich Village basement apartment in order to gather material for Jeff's new book.

programme.title: You Can't Run Away From It
programme.desc: A spoiled heiress teams up with an enterprising reporter to escape her domineering father's plan to keep her from getting married.

programme.title: The Notorious Landlady
programme.desc: An American junior diplomat in London rents a house from, and falls in love with, a woman suspected of murder.

programme.title: M
programme.desc: When the police in a German city are unable to catch a child-murderer, other criminals join in the manhunt.

programme.title: Dead Reckoning
programme.desc: A thriller inspired by the Boston Marathon bombing in 2013.

programme.title: Misbehaviour
programme.desc: A group of women hatch a plan to disrupt the 1970 Miss World beauty competition in London.

programme.title: Silent Hill: Revelation
programme.desc: Return to a place of insanity and blood-curdling chills in this shocking sequel to one of the most surreal and gruesome horror films ever created. Heather Mason (Adelaide Clemens) and her father (Sean Bean) have always tried to stay one step ahead of the malevolent forces intent on their destruction. But on the eve of her 18th birthday, a dangerous revelation leads her deeper into a demonic world that threatens to trap her in a nightmarish landscape forever.

programme.title: The Company of Wolves
programme.desc: A teenage girl in a country manor falls asleep while reading a magazine, and has a disturbing dream involving wolves prowling the woods below her bedroom window.

programme.title: Robin Hood
programme.desc: An action-packed retelling of the classic legend, filled with thrilling battle sequences and epic adventure.

programme.title: Empire State
programme.desc: Two childhood friends plan to rob an armored-car depository, but an NYPD officer stands in their way.

programme.title: Tracers
programme.desc: Taylor Lautner stars in a pulse-pounding action heist film set in the world of parkour.

programme.title: Pursuit
programme.desc: A troubled cop hunts for a ruthless hacker, who’s committing violence as he searches for his kidnapped wife.

programme.title: Joe Baby
programme.desc: A money collector for a private investigator is hired to track down 10 million dollars stolen in a dodgy real estate deal, opening a complex can of worms.

programme.title: Prey of Wrath
programme.desc: An elite special forces operative is sent by Russian Intelligence to hunt down former teammates who have hijacked nuclear warheads.

programme.title: The Wide West
programme.desc: 1907. A mysterious gambler aids a couple of Japanese sumo wrestlers, the local Judge and a beautiful store owner against ruthless outlaws who have attacked a small Californian town.

programme.title: Reawakening
programme.desc: John and Mary have lived in grief for ten years because their child, Clare, disappeared when she was fourteen. When Clare mysteriously returns, John suspects she's not who she claims to be, plunging the couple into a search for the truth.

programme.title: Salvation
programme.desc: A foster child bonds with a snake-handling Pentecostal community but uncovers sinister truths. She must choose between reporting pastor's crimes or preserving her newfound support system, risking personal safety for a sense of belonging.

programme.title: Shaolin
programme.desc: After a shocking betrayal by his sworn brother, a general (Andy Lau) takes refuge at a Shaolin temple and learns martial arts from a crazy cook (Jackie Chan).

programme.title: Alienoid
programme.desc: Gurus in the late Goryeo dynasty try to obtain a fabled, holy sword, and humans in 2022 hunt down an alien prisoner that is locked in a human's body. The two parties cross paths when a time-traveling portal opens up.

programme.title: The Brink
programme.desc: A reckless police inspector is on a mission to crack down on a criminal's gold smuggling scheme.

programme.title: Sword Master
programme.desc: A master swordsman must once again wield his weapon against those who threaten his desire to live in peace.

programme.title: Jodi Arias: Dirty Little Secret
programme.desc: Based on the disturbing murder trial that has captivated the nation.

programme.title: My Family's Killer Affairs
programme.desc: Nikki is shocked to meet Jenell, the daughter she never knew.

programme.title: Cellmate Secrets
programme.desc: Casey Anthony's former jail mate reveals how the two forged a deep friendship.

programme.title: A Killer in My Home
programme.desc: Allison Winters' life crumbles as husband dies, exposing a secret family.

programme.title: Jodi Arias: Dirty Little Secret
programme.desc: Based on the disturbing murder trial that has captivated the nation.

programme.title: My Family's Killer Affairs
programme.desc: Nikki is shocked to meet Jenell, the daughter she never knew.

programme.title: Best Friend From Heaven
programme.desc: When her dog is killed, Tara cancels her wedding. But her dog's ghost and her small town have a surprise.

programme.title: Let Love Grow
programme.desc: Landscape architect Evie meets her match with new client Ryan, whose daughters are her biggest fan.

programme.title: Inspiring Love
programme.desc: When Isla’s art unexpectedly sells to Mark, a second commission sees them falling in love.

programme.title: FBI: Negotiator
programme.desc: An FBI agent must negotiate with a woman holding a hospital hostage in order to get her hands on an experimental drug.

programme.title: Angel and the Bad Man
programme.desc: A notorious gunfighter renounces violence upon falling in love with a Quaker woman, but must pick up his guns again when an old foe returns.

programme.title: The Challenger
programme.desc: The Challenger follows the life struggles of a young, Bronx auto mechanic, Jaden Miller. When Jaden and his mother are evicted from their home, he soon finds that boxing may be the key to stop them from living on the streets and the ticket to a better life. With legendary trainer, Duane Taylor, Miller becomes a local hero and captures the attention of a cable network that decides to create a reality show around his goal to fight to the Light-Heavyweight Title.

programme.title: The Climb
programme.desc: The breath-taking story of two men ... one with a courageous heart who seeks God; the other with an independent spirit who relies on his own strength.

programme.title: Take Back
programme.desc: Zara (Gillian White) and Brian (Michael Jai White) are living the perfect small town life when a mysterious stranger from Zara's secret past kidnaps their daughter and now they must race to save her life.

programme.title: On Angel's Wings
programme.desc: When an angel grants a struggling water girl (Taylor Faye Ruffin) three wishes, even her mom (Robin Givens) or coach (Reginald VelJohnson) can't figure out how she became a soccer superstar.

programme.title: I'll Be There
programme.desc: More than a decade after distancing herself from her complicated family, Grace's carefully built life is upended when her brother AJ is diagnosed with cancer. As her estranged siblings reunite in Los Angeles, Grace is forced to confront old wounds, buried loyalties, and the role she once played in her fractured family-set against the surreal backdrop of Michael Jackson's death.

programme.title: Played in My Face
programme.desc: Tonya’s life is almost picture perfect, and a wedding on the horizon to the man of her dreams, Victor. One night she receives an anonymous email exposing Victor at a hotel on the same night he claimed to be away on business. Her quiet suspicion spirals into obsession as she discovers that Victor’s betrayal runs deeper than she ever imagined.

programme.title: My Plus Size Bachelorette
programme.desc: Symone, a spirited and vibrant plus-size woman in her 30s, finds herself navigating the unpredictable world of modern dating while grappling with the emotional aftermath of her one-night stand with Andre, her high school sweetheart. With her friends rallying around her, they encourage Symone to embrace her single status and embark on a journey of self-discovery and love.

programme.title: False Profits
programme.desc: Delilah is imprisoned for fraud after being apart of her family’s gospel group which was a front for a ponzi scheme. She agrees to be interviewed by a local reporter about her past and her side of the crime. On the day of the interview, her sister performs a song hinting at hidden truths about her past.

programme.title: Don't Miss the Bus
programme.desc: Sierra, a single mother hustles in order to survive with long shifts and grunt work. Her faith is pinned on lottery tickets and men who exploit her vulnerability. When a stroke of luck has the ability to change her future forever, a missed bus stop could mean life or death.

programme.title: 5-Headed Shark Attack
programme.desc: A monster 5-Headed Shark terrorizes the beaches of Puerto Rico, endangering the once peaceful island paradise.

programme.title: Mega Python vs Gatoroid
programme.desc: When a species of giant python invades Florida, park rangers breed mutant alligators to counter the threat.

programme.title: Mega Piranha
programme.desc: A mutant strain of giant ferocious piranha escape from the Amazon and eat their way toward Florida.

programme.title: Mega Shark vs Crocosaurus
programme.desc: Surviving the last battle, a massive prehistoric shark is threatened by a new foe discovered in the jungle.

programme.title: Megaboa
programme.desc: College students look for cave drawings in Columbia, but find a once-extinct and deadly 50-foot boa constrictor.

programme.title: The Deeper You Dig (Edited Version)
programme.desc: 14-year old Echo and her mother Ivy, a tarot card reader, live a quiet life in a rural area. When reclusive Kurt moves down the road to restore an abandoned farmhouse, an accident leads to Echos murder, and suddenly three lives collide in mysterious and wicked ways. Kurt assumes he can hide his secret under the ground. But Echo burrows into his head until he can feel her in his bones. As she haunts his every move, trying to reach her mother from beyond, Ivy must dig deep to see the signs and prove that love wont stay buried.

programme.title: The Funhouse Massacre (Edited Version)
programme.desc: Six of the world's scariest psychopaths escape from a local Asylum and proceed to unleash terror on the unsuspecting crowd of a Halloween Funhouse whose themed mazes are inspired by their various reigns of terror.

programme.title: Babysitter Must Die
programme.desc: A wealthy family's home is invaded during a game of hide-and-seek by a pack cultists. Josie, the babysitter hides in plain sight prepared and armed with a handful of survival skills. She battles the cult and her fears through a long night of terror. Ultimately good defeats evil and she triumphs, but not before a cruel twist reveals the cult were just foot soldiers for a much greater evil.

programme.title: Mermaid Down
programme.desc: A mermaid is ripped from the Pacific, her tail is chopped off and she's thrown into a mental home where no one believes she is a mermaid.

programme.title: The Deeper You Dig (Edited Version)
programme.desc: 14-year old Echo and her mother Ivy, a tarot card reader, live a quiet life in a rural area. When reclusive Kurt moves down the road to restore an abandoned farmhouse, an accident leads to Echos murder, and suddenly three lives collide in mysterious and wicked ways. Kurt assumes he can hide his secret under the ground. But Echo burrows into his head until he can feel her in his bones. As she haunts his every move, trying to reach her mother from beyond, Ivy must dig deep to see the signs and prove that love wont stay buried.

programme.title: Horror Noire
programme.desc: Horror Noire

programme.title: Ghost Stories
programme.desc: A man looking to debunk a series of paranormal events falls into a world of terror. Martin Freeman stars in a tale that will haunt your dreams.

programme.title: Babadook, The
programme.desc: In this terrifying thriller, a mother must protect her son from an evil, supernatural entity that has escaped the pages of a children's book and is lurking in their home.

programme.title: Come True
programme.desc: Looking for an escape from her recurring nightmares, 18-year-old Sarah submits to a university sleep study, but soon realizes she's become the conduit to a frightening new discovery.

programme.title: The Beverly Hillbillies
programme.desc: "Hair-Raising Holiday" - After Granny cuts off the top of Drysdale and Clyburn's (his doctor) hair for some homegrown remedy, Clyburn tries to keep her from ever doctoring again. Meanwhile, the Clampetts are looking forward to the Possum Day parade, which of course isn't celebrated in Beverly Hills, so Drysdale has to figure out how to have one to keep them happy.

programme.title: The Beverly Hillbillies
programme.desc: "Jed Gets the Misery" - Jed cheers up Granny by pretending to be sick so she can doctor him. Soon Drysdale and his doctor, Clyburn, are subject to her methods as well.

programme.title: The Beverly Hillbillies
programme.desc: Jethro brings home a friend, Armstrong Dueser McHugh III, who is coddled by a chauffeur who treats him as frail. The Clampetts know better, and show him a good time, friendship and family.

programme.title: The Beverly Hillbillies
programme.desc: When Miss Hathaway gets sick and Mr. Drysdale has to speak at a conference, Jed helps out by running the bank and having Elly be his secretary, while Jethro finds a speech that Drysdale accidentally uses instead of his own. All the while a new teller sucks-up to the men and flirts with the women.

programme.title: The Beverly Hillbillies
programme.desc: "The Psychiatrist Gets Clampetted" - Jethro finally gets his bill of health from Dr. Twombly so he can graduate the fifth grade, but Twombly really wants to talk to Grannie again. Drysdale brings him to the Clampetts' just as Grannie showed Pearl how to use a love potion, so everyone thinks Twombly's interest in Grannie is love.

programme.title: The Beverly Hillbillies
programme.desc: "The Clampetts Get Psychoanalyzed" - Jethro needs a health certificate to graduate from the fifth grade, so he goes to the only doctor the Clampetts know about - Mrs. Drysdale's psychiatrist. He gets nowhere with Jethro, so Pearl comes and she thinks he is testing her virtue when he tries to lay her on his couch.

programme.title: The Beverly Hillbillies
programme.desc: "The Clampetts in Court" - When a couple who ran into the Clampett's car discover that they're rich, they fake injuries and sue the Clampetts.

programme.title: The Beverly Hillbillies
programme.desc: "The Clampetts Entertain" - Jed arranges a party to get Grannie out of her doldrums and invites the Drysdale. But when Mr. Drysdale's boss comes and wants to meet them for dinner, he wonders if he's going to be sent to an Alaskan bank when his boss finds out he's been lying about Jed and his family being sophisticated.

programme.title: The Beverly Hillbillies
programme.desc: "Duke Becomes a Father" - Mlle. Denise comes back for the birth of her dog's puppies, and to see Jed. They do some "courtin' and sparkin'" Mrs. Drysdale isn't nearly so happy when she finds the puppies share more in common with Duke the bloodhound than Claude the poodle.

programme.title: The Beverly Hillbillies
programme.desc: "The Clampetts and the Dodgers" - Jed and Jethro go golfing with Leo Durocher, coach of the Los Angeles Dodgers, who wants to recruit Jethro as a pitcher when he sees how well he can throw.

programme.title: The Beverly Hillbillies
programme.desc: "Jed Pays His Income Tax" - When an IRS agent gets chased away by Granny, Mr. Drysdale tells him the story of how the Clampetts came to be rich and move to Beverly Hills.

programme.title: The Beverly Hillbillies
programme.desc: "Granny's Spring Tonic" - Miss Hathaway's brilliant-but-frumpy bank protegee Gloria is really a sultry gold-digger with eyes for Jed's $34 million, and Jed's just had his annual dose of Granny's spring tonic.

programme.title: The Beverly Hillbillies
programme.desc: "Jed Cuts the Family Tree" - Mrs. Drysdale continues to be forced into doing "practices from the past" by Mrs. Smith-Standish, who discovers that Jed's family is possibly the first to come the country. If so they'd be famous worldwide, but does Jed want that?

programme.title: The Beverly Hillbillies
programme.desc: "The Family Tree" - Mrs. Drysdale wants to get rid of the Clampetts before the arrival of Mrs. Smith-Standish, the head of a 'first family' historical society. But Mrs. Smith-Standish goes to the Clampetts' first and becomes enchanted with their antiques and way of life, even to roping the horrified Mrs. Drysdale into helping do the chores the old-fashioned way.

programme.title: The Beverly Hillbillies
programme.desc: "Jed Becomes a Banker" - Drysdale hires Jed to be a banker when he needs a crack shot to help beat a rival banker in a skeet shooting competition, but his partner needs to be an employee.

programme.title: The Rifleman
programme.desc: The lazy Jackman brothers mortgage has been foreclosed. Unfit for farming, they head to a nearby town.

programme.title: The Rifleman
programme.desc: Lucas and Mark decide to enter a local turkey shoot to raise money to buy a ranch.

programme.title: The Rifleman
programme.desc: Cattle baron Oat Jackford orders his two cowboys to run Lucas McCain and his son off their ranch.

programme.title: The Rifleman
programme.desc: When Mark gets caught on a mountain ledge, a young outlaw risks his life to rescue him and breaks his leg doing so.

programme.title: The Rifleman
programme.desc: The notorious Shelton rides into the town of North Fork to take revenge on an elderly Marshal who is now crippled and working for Lucas McCain.

programme.title: The Rifleman
programme.desc: A vengeful judge who believes Lucas was responsible for his son's death, kidnaps Mark McCain to revenge himself on Lucas.

programme.title: The Rifleman
programme.desc: A handsome Italian count arrives in North Fork. The town bully ridicules his manner of speech and dress and provokes the Count to challenge him to a duel.

programme.title: The Rifleman

programme.desc: Jim Bruer's Heavy Metal Comedy was born on December 9th, 2000 when Jim unleashed his Rock-n-Comedy assault on an unsuspecting, standing room only crowd at New York City's, Irving Plaza. New routines such as Coming Home at 4am have already become cult faves and Bruer favorites like The Party have received a musical facelift. Jim's uncanny impressions, such as Brian Johnson of AC/DC, are also included.

programme.title: David Heti: It Was Ok
programme.desc: With a sly, biting wit, Heti's truths cut close to the bone, hewing to reality in all its complexity and madness.

programme.title: Mike Stanley: Salty Language, Peppered Morals
programme.desc: An alternately serious and silly documentary short gives a backstage look at comics' life on the road.

programme.title: Live at The Stand: Passed
programme.desc: See what up and comers have passed on to become headliners at The Stand NYC.

programme.title: Glenn Wool: You Don't Go To Hell For Eating Elephants
programme.desc: With whimsical trills and unexpected intonations, Glenn Wool's precision high-low comedy captivates but does not coddle.

programme.title: Graham Kay: Stupid Jokes
programme.desc: Graham Kay delivers observational humor and sarcastic charm, taking on everyday topics from surviving a breakup in New York City to tracing-paper pornography.

programme.title: Maria Bamford: Plan B
programme.desc: Maria Bamford lovingly skewers her origins, reminding us that there's really no place like home.

programme.title: Upstairs @ The Stand
programme.desc: Checkout the newest up and comers making big waves on the Upstairs stage of NYC’s famous The Stand.

programme.title: Jimmy O. Yang: Good Deal
programme.desc: In his debut stand-up special Jimmy will tell you all about his take on Asian representation.

programme.title: Craig Ferguson: I'm Here To Help
programme.desc: Craig Ferguson unleashes his trademark stream-of-consciousness comedy before a sold-out crowd.

programme.title: Rob Schneider: Soy Sauce and the Holocaust
programme.desc: He tells the side-splitting stories of the three hoods: childhood, adulthood, and parenthood.

programme.title: Jeff Allen: Honor Thy Wife
programme.desc: Jeff Allen's Dry Bar Comedy videos have amassed millions of views on social media and other platforms.

programme.title: Jeff Allen: I Can Laugh About it Now
programme.desc: Jeff Allen combines clean, hilarious humor like no other comedian working today.

programme.title: Jim Breuer… And Laughter For All
programme.desc: No longer a metal head, a stoner or a Goat Boy.

programme.title: Jim Breuer: Let's Clear The Air
programme.desc: The SNL alum, radio host, and film actor clears the air at his live event.

programme.title: Take Me Home Tonight
programme.desc: Four years after graduation, an awkward high school genius uses his sister's boyfriend's Labor Day party

programme.title: The Little Hours
programme.desc: In the Middle Ages, a young servant fleeing from his master takes refuge.

programme.title: Coffee And Cigarettes
programme.desc: A series of vignettes that all have coffee and cigarettes in common.

programme.title: Learning To Drive
programme.desc: As her marriage dissolves, a Manhattan writer takes driving lessons from a Sikh instructor with his own marriage troubles.

programme.title: Chewed Up
programme.desc: Michael Symon, Carla Hall, and Clinton Kelly welcome acclaimed Miami chef Michelle Bernstein to a special Miami Beach episode of Chewed Up filmed at the W Hotel in partnership with Experience Miami Beach. After sampling local bakery favorites including guava pastries and tequeños, the hosts celebrate Miami’s vibrant culinary scene and discuss Michelle’s lasting impact on South Florida food culture as she shares stories about her career, mentorship, and being honored during the South Beach Wine & Food Festival’s 25th anniversary celebration. Michelle then teaches the group how to make her signature Cuban sandwich empanadas, transforming the classic Miami staple into a crispy handheld pastry filled with lechón, ham, cheese, pickles, and yellow mustard. As they assemble and fry their empanadas, the conversation ranges from Cuban sandwich authenticity and Latin food traditions to family roots and restaurant life. The group also plays a lively round of “Chew It or Screw It,” debating topics such as late dinner reservations, over-tipping, flip-flops at fine dining restaurants, poolside rosé, and afternoon Cuban coffee. During Fan Mail, they answer viewer questions about cooking with candied jalapeños, discuss which decade they would most like to have grown up in, and share their favorite ways to unwind after a long day. The episode wraps with the hosts tasting Michelle’s empanadas, praising the crisp dough and balanced flavors, while Michelle shares details about her new restaurant, La Cañita. Two audience giveaways are awarded before the hosts encourage viewers to experience everything Miami Beach has to offer.

programme.title: Chewed Up
programme.desc: Clinton Kelly takes the lead in a conversation all about meal prepping and healthy breakfast habits while showing Carla Hall and Michael Symon his simple overnight oats recipe. Clinton explains how prepping breakfast the night before has helped him make smarter food choices and stay on track with his health goals, sharing that he has lost 14 pounds through portion control and calorie awareness without giving up the foods he loves. As he combines oats, oat milk, and chia seeds into mason jars, the hosts joke about everything from “wet walnuts” and frozen yogurt nostalgia to chia pets and old-school diet culture. Carla adds her perspective on whole foods and how certain ingredients help keep people full longer, while Michael balances the conversation with humor and practical advice about calories, exercise, and understanding how different foods affect the body. The discussion expands into smart scales, fitness misconceptions, and the importance of focusing on overall wellness instead of obsessing over a single number on the scale. Later in the episode, the hosts answer fan mail questions about making homemade pet food and whether they prefer performing in front of live audiences or taped studio environments, reflecting on their experiences filming at food festivals and live events. The episode wraps with the announcement of the Fan of the Day and sponsor mentions for Fresh Direct and Frontgate.

programme.title: Chewed Up
programme.desc: Michael Symon revisits one of the most important dishes of his career as he makes his famous rotisserie chicken mac and cheese, a creamy pasta recipe that first became a hit at his Cleveland restaurant Piccolo Mondo in 1992. Using rigatoni, heavy cream, goat cheese, fresh rosemary, and shredded rotisserie chicken, Michael walks Clinton Kelly and Carla Hall through the deceptively simple recipe while sharing stories from his early days as a 22-year-old executive chef running a packed live-fire kitchen. The conversation turns nostalgic as Michael explains how the dish followed him from restaurant to restaurant and became such a customer favorite that he eventually had to remove it from the menu. The hosts laugh over Michael’s son Kyle unknowingly eating goat cheese for years despite insisting he hated it, while Clinton passionately defends rosemary after recalling a social media argument about the herb. The episode also features sponsor shout-outs to Fresh Direct and Frontgate, plus playful banter about portion sizes, “fat soup diets,” and airplane etiquette after eating rich pasta dishes. Once plated, the hosts rave about the comforting flavors, especially how the chicken melts into the creamy sauce and how the rosemary balances the tanginess of the goat cheese. The episode wraps with the selection of Melicia from New York as the “Fan of the Day,” followed by more joking between the hosts as they finish off the rich and nostalgic dish.

programme.title: Chewed Up
programme.desc: Clinton Kelly leads this Chewed Up Bites episode by transforming leftover rotisserie chicken into a colorful, crunchy chicken salad inspired by the classic Chinese chicken salad popularized by Wolfgang Puck’s Chinois. As he assembles a simple sesame-lime vinaigrette and tosses together cabbage, carrots, bok choy, bell peppers, scallions, and shredded chicken, the hosts discuss the value of leftovers, pantry staples, and the difference between a salad and simply a bowl of lettuce. Michael Symon shares thoughts on menu development and simple comfort foods like peanut butter and jelly sandwiches. The conversation evolves into a broader discussion about healthy eating habits, meal planning, and the realization that not every meal needs to be an indulgent experience. Along the way, the hosts joke about Clinton’s “Gay Soup Diet,” debate the merits of themed ingredient weeks, and exchange playful banter about fashion makeovers, aging, and personal style. Sponsor shout-outs to FreshDirect and Frontgate are woven into the episode, highlighting convenient grocery delivery and entertaining essentials. A fan mail segment sparks more comedy, including the possibility of a future Gay Soup Diet cookbook. The episode wraps with the hosts sampling the finished salad, celebrating its fresh flavors and nutritional balance, before signing off with thanks to viewers and listeners.

programme.title: Chewed Up
programme.desc: In this episode of Chewed Up Show, hosts Clinton Kelly, Carla Hall, and Michael Symon transform rotisserie chicken into Buffalo Chicken Cornbread Melts, the grand finale of Leftover Chicken Week. Carla Hall shares her kitchen genius by combining hot sauce, butter, and shredded rotisserie chicken with a crisp celery blue cheese slaw, served on toasted cornbread rectangles. The hosts discuss ingredient swaps, blue cheese preferences, and techniques for mellowing raw onions. The episode showcases how classic wing flavors elevate simple leftovers into an elegant, less-messy alternative to traditional buffalo wings. The hosts also engage with fan mail, discussing rice rinsing preferences and brainstorming potential spin-off show concepts.

programme.title: Chewed Up
programme.desc: On Father's Day, Michael Symon and Clinton Kelly are joined by social media cooking sensation Ereka Vetrini to create quick, delicious dishes perfect for summer entertaining. Vetrini, who built a massive following on TikTok and Instagram during the pandemic, shares her approach to accessible Italian-inspired cooking. The episode features two recipes: roasted red pepper bruschetta with Calabrian chili mayo and a perfectly seared 20-minute tuna steak topped with a caper-shallot relish. Between cooking segments, the hosts discuss parenting, picky eaters, navigating social media criticism, and the importance of real food over processed trends. Vetrini shares her philosophy that cooking at home is about making people feel loved and special.

programme.title: Chewed Up
programme.desc: In this Father's Day-themed episode of Chewed Up Show, Michael Symon takes the lead in the kitchen preparing grilled pork chops finished with a homemade cherry-bourbon gastrique—a classic French-Italian sweet and sour sauce. Guest host Ereka Vetrini joins returning host Clinton Kelly as they discuss family dining traditions, Michael's father's grilling mishaps, and the importance of proper meat preparation. The episode covers essential cooking tips including salting meat in advance, resting temperatures, and the balance of fat and acid in great sauces. The team also demonstrates the Tumbler knife sharpener and discusses Italian-American family customs around entertaining and basement kitchens.

programme.title: Chewed Up
programme.desc: Michael Symon and Clinton Kelly welcome culinary producer Allison Cancro as a guest co-host while Carla Hall is performing her one-woman show. The team creates an affordable sausage and peppers dish with corn and tomato salad, keeping the total cost under $25 for four people. Allison shares her Jersey Italian heritage and family food traditions, while the hosts discuss farmer's markets, fresh produce, and regional ingredients. A video segment features Carla Hall preparing bone-in pork chops with smashed sweet potatoes. The episode concludes with a Price Is Right-style pricing game featuring grocery items.

programme.title: Chewed Up
programme.desc: Michael Symon and Clinton Kelly kick off July 4th week with guest Jeanette Donnarumma, a returning favorite from the Rachael Ray Show who pioneered the "Watermelon Palooza" segment. Together they prepare summer cookout essentials: Watermelon Aperol Margaritas with frozen fruit ice, a Watermelon Cucumber Salad served in an artfully carved watermelon bowl, and taste-test limited-edition Lay's World Cup chips. The episode celebrates simple, accessible recipes perfect for backyard gatherings, with Donnarumma sharing pro tips on watermelon carving and ingredient selection while the hosts discuss recipe inspiration and kitchen mood-boosters.

programme.title: Chewed Up
programme.desc: Michael Symon joins Clinton Kelly and Stacy London for a July Fourth-themed episode where he teaches the notoriously non-cooking Stacy how to grill skirt steak with a quick charred corn salsa featuring tomatillos, jalapeños, and garlic. The episode showcases fundamental cooking techniques including seasoning, temperature control, and proper resting. Stacy surprises the group with an air fryer and attempts to make dairy-free s'mores using gluten-free cookies and specialty peanut butter cups, leading to comedic mishaps. The hosts also discuss their new show "Why'd They Wear That," where they critique character wardrobes from films and television. The episode emphasizes that cooking is about patience, proper technique, and feeding people you care about.

programme.title: Chewed Up
programme.desc: Michael Symon, Clinton Kelly, and Carla Hall welcome renowned chef Marco Canora, founder of Brodo bone broth and executive chef of Hearth restaurant in New York City. Canora shares how broth became central to his wellness journey and cooking philosophy. The hosts and guest prepare a roasted half-chicken with a pan sauce made from brodo, demonstrating how quality broth elevates home cooking. The episode includes a NYC food-themed "Chew It or Screw It" game and explores Canora's approach to seasonal, farm-to-table cooking. Canora reveals creative applications of broth, including savory beverage variations with olive oil, bacon fat, and other ingredients.

programme.title: Simply Symons
programme.desc: In the debut episode of Simply Symons, Michael Symon welcomes viewers into his Venice, California front yard alongside his wife Liz Symon and their friend Olivia Annacone Cisic for an unfiltered, laid-back cookout. Celebrating Cinco de Mayo, Michael prepares simple yet flavorful black cod fish tacos on a plancha, walking through his approach to seasoning, cooking fish by sight, and building a bright, crunchy slaw with cabbage, citrus, and charred poblanos. The conversation flows easily as the trio shares cooking tips, debates ingredients like corn versus flour tortillas, and jokes about knife sharpening habits, cilantro preferences, and home cooking realities. Liz complements the meal with a refreshing jalapeño margarita, balancing heat with fresh lime juice, simple syrup, and a hint of orange liqueur, while adding her own creative twist with lemon and bitters. Throughout the episode, the group emphasizes flexibility in cooking—encouraging viewers to adjust flavors, garnishes, and spice levels to their own tastes. Lighthearted banter continues as they assemble tacos, discuss plating ideas, and highlight optional additions like crema and avocado. The episode wraps with a taste test, enthusiastic reactions to both the tacos and cocktail, and a reminder that great food doesn’t have to be complicated—just fresh, simple, and made with joy.

programme.title: Simply Symons
programme.desc: Michael Symon, Liz Symon, and Olivia Annacone Cisic tackle a brunch-inspired twist on eggs Benedict by swapping traditional Canadian bacon for crispy pastrami and pairing it with a mustard-spiked hollandaise sauce. Michael walks through a foolproof blender hollandaise method while explaining the science behind emulsifying butter and keeping the sauce warm, making the intimidating brunch staple feel approachable for home cooks. As the family cooks together, the conversation bounces between Olivia’s skepticism about poached eggs, Liz’s love of runny yolks, and memories of iconic pastrami spots like Langer’s and Katz’s. The group also debates poaching techniques, from classic whirlpool methods to muffin tin hacks for feeding a crowd. Humor carries the episode throughout as Michael teases Olivia’s picky eating habits and the family jokes about “buttering muffins” and hollandaise disasters. Liz complements the rich brunch dish with a citrus-forward mimosa made from blood oranges, sumo oranges, lemon juice, and frozen strawberries used as flavorful ice cubes. Once plated, the pastrami Benedict wins Olivia over completely, with the crispy pastrami and tangy mustard hollandaise becoming the standout combination. The episode wraps with the family enjoying brunch cocktails, discussing make-ahead egg tips, and Michael reminding viewers that restaurant-quality brunch can come together quickly and simply at home.

programme.title: Simply Symons
programme.desc: Michael Symon kicks off a new era of Simply Symons from Venice, California, joined by Liz Symon and Olivia “Liv” Annacone Cisic for a laid-back backyard cookout centered around the ultimate burger debate: smash burgers versus classic pub burgers. Michael walks through the techniques behind each style, from selecting the ideal fat ratio for ground beef to achieving the perfect crust on a hot grill and cast iron griddle. Along the way, the trio shares stories about old Symon’s Dinners episodes, Liv’s transition into motherhood, and the realities of filming outdoors with planes overhead, lawn equipment buzzing nearby, and neighborhood life unfolding around them. As Michael builds both burgers with grilled onions, pickles, secret sauce, and different cheeses, the conversation turns into a humorous discussion about condiments, cooking preferences, smash burger myths, and burger nostalgia. Liz keeps the energy going with playful jabs at Michael while also preparing his favorite cocktail, the bourbon-based Paper Plane, complete with Aperol, Amaro, and fresh lemon juice. The episode wraps with a tasting session comparing both burgers side-by-side, with everyone weighing in on which version wins the battle. Between the casual banter, cooking tips, and family-style atmosphere, the episode delivers an easygoing and highly approachable backyard burger feast.

programme.title: Behind the Counter with David Chang
programme.desc: Behind The Counter With Dave Chang: The Spring Menu - An unfiltered behind-the-scenes look into David Chang's kitchen as he and his team develop and practice a unique menu in the days leading up to the next Netflix episode of Dinner Time Live. This episode sees Dave working on a Springtime inspired menu for guests Maya Rudolph and Ron Funchess. Dishes include: pea and mint soup, truffled egg toast, crab cakes with warmed artichoke and ramp salad, and roast lamb chops with spätzle and braised morels.

programme.title: Behind the Counter with David Chang
programme.desc: Behind the Counter - The Vegan Korean Menu: This week, Dave is developing a vegan Korean menu for guests Nikki Glaser and Pete Holmes. Dishes include: Banchan, the Korean name for side dishes served with rice; fried mushroom Tangsuyuk; crispy bindaetteok cones; avocado rice; kongguksu; and Asian pear shaved ice.

programme.title: Ying Cooks the Internet
programme.desc: "Ratatouille Tarte Tatin" - Chris Ying creates a beautiful ratatouille tarte Tatin, motivated by a combination of online influences, including an on-the-fly challenge from his buddy David Chang while they were making chicken pot pie together in a different video. Chris riffs on the popular trend zipping around social media to make tarte Tatin (typically a sweet pastry) while paying homage to recipe YouTuber extraordinaire, Andrew Rea (Binging with Babish), who's first mega-popular video was recreating the ratatouille from...Ratatouille.

programme.title: Ying Cooks the Internet
programme.desc: "WTF is BORG and Can a Mixologist Make it Good?" - Professional bartender, mixologist and author, John deBary, joins Chris Ying to figure out why all the college kids are going bonkers over a viral drink trend called BORG (Black Out Rage Gallon). Yikes! Is it any good? Are the kids maybe onto something here? Can John come up with a recipe that elevates it from the bottom shelf to...a slightly higher shelf? We feel old.

programme.title: Ying Cooks the Internet
programme.desc: "McDonald's General Tso's Chicken" - Chris Ying has seen all of the McDonald's "hacks" that are floating around social media, and he's calling BS. Most of these don't even qualify as actual hacks, so he takes matters into his own hands and concocts a clever dish using Chicken McNuggets, Sweet & Sour sauce, ketchup, (and a few other things you'd have in your kitchen) to truly upgrade your McDonald's meal.

programme.title: Ying Cooks the Internet
programme.desc: "A Complete Christmas Roast Beef Dinner" - The internet is full of people advertising their recipes for affordable! simple! budget-buster! Christmas dinners. But just because you want to save some money and not break your back making dinner doesn't mean you don't want something festive and celebratory. Chris Ying is here to light the way to a full Christmas dinner—roast beef, horseradish cream, creamed spinach, baked potatoes, Yorkshire pudding—for under $50. Nothing hacky or gimmick-y about it. Just a good old-fashioned heart-warming holiday dinner from Colonel Christmas himself.

programme.title: The Dave Chang Cooking Show
programme.desc: Dave and Chris Ying sit down to work out a menu for this week's guest in the studio: artisanal baker, New Yorker by way of New Orleans, allergic-to-shellfish friend of the show, Bryan Ford.

programme.title: The Dave Chang Cooking Show
programme.desc: With a New Orleans–inspired menu now set for his friend Bryan Ford, Dave starts testing out this week’s recipes: barbecue turkey legs, meat pies, coconut flatbread, and coconut beignets.

programme.desc: Emeril prepares dishes with saffron grown right in the southern United States.

programme.title: Tasting History with Max Miller
programme.desc: Max explores Titanic’s luxurious first class, from modest suites to lavish parlor suites with private promenades. He covers dining in the saloon and Ritz, daily meals, amenities like the gym and Turkish bath, and the 10+ course final dinner. Using survivor accounts, he recreates the famous peaches in Chartreuse jelly dessert from April 14, 1912.

programme.title: Tasting History with Max Miller
programme.desc: Max explores Titanic’s first class breakfast from April 11, 1912, featuring over a dozen dishes by Escoffier. He recreates baked apples, smoked salmon coronets, shirred eggs, and buckwheat cakes with blackcurrant conserve. The episode highlights dining venues, passenger pets, and the tragic story of honeymooners Victor and Maria Josefa Peñasco.

programme.title: Tasting History with Max Miller
programme.desc: Max explores the 4–6 month, 2,200-mile Oregon Trail journey (1846–1869), covering provisions like flour, bacon, and hardtack, cooking over buffalo chips, and staple meals like Johnny cakes. He discusses hunting, trading with Native Americans, celebrations, and hardships, including spoiled food, salt-rising bread, pies, and pioneers leaving possessions at Fort Laramie.

programme.title: Tasting History with Max Miller
programme.desc: Max recreates Ötzi the Iceman’s last meal (c. 3,359–3,105 BC), discovered in 1991. He covers Ötzi’s clothing, tools, health (tattoos, heart disease, parasites), and likely murder by arrow. Using stomach contents—smoked ibex, charred red deer, and einkorn pancake with fiddle ferns—Max cooks over an open fire to mimic Neolithic methods.

programme.title: Tasting History with Max Miller
programme.desc: Max traces airline food from 1919’s first commercial meal to aviation’s Golden Age (1950s–70s). He covers flight kitchens, cold-to-hot meal evolution, themed flights, flight attendant hiring, the 1958 Great Sandwich War, and 1978 deregulation. Max recreates a 1954 United Airlines meal of pot roast, vegetables, Delmonico potatoes, and marshmallow-apple salad.

programme.title: Tasting History with Max Miller
programme.desc: Max recreates Medieval Irish corned beef and cabbage from *The Vision of Mac Con Glinne* (c. 1100) with onion-leek pottage. He explores Ireland’s rigid food hierarchy—peasants ate grains and salted meat, butter signified wealth, monks preferred game, nobles had fresh meat—and cultural practices like fosterage, the 7-year child-rearing system, and a satirical poem about King Cathal’s gluttony.

programme.title: Tasting History with Max Miller
programme.desc: Max explores lighthouse keeper life via an 1881 provisioning list and period recipes. He covers the isolated, demanding job—cleaning Fresnel lenses with mercury, trimming wicks, and cooking with limited supplies like flour, beans, and salt pork. Featuring keepers Henry Long, Mary Ryan, and Hannah Thomas, he recreates Fannie Farmer’s 1896 Boston corn chowder with pilot bread.

programme.title: Ciao Italia
programme.desc: Mary Ann teaches her Scuola Culinaria student, Karen the traditional Italian culinary secrets of creating soft-as-a-cloud, pillowy gnocchi every time. When they’re as perfect as these Amalfi-style gnocchi, all that’s needed is a buttery, heavy cream sauce of asparagus tips and lemon zest to make you close your eyes, take one taste and swear you’re in Amalfi.

programme.title: Ciao Italia
programme.desc: In a matter of minutes, Mary Ann combines sliced chicken breasts with onions, garlic, and turmeric and into the oven it goes to create her husband’s recipe Guy’s Chicken with Olives and Turmeric. Then she makes another favorite, Meatballs Milan Style. Stuffed with filled with mortadella, beef, and ham and fried to a crunchy crisp, you’ll want seconds, thirds, fourths!

programme.title: Ciao Italia
programme.desc: Mary Ann and eager Scuola Culinaria student John roll up their sleeves and dive into the essential steps need to create an all-time favorite of favorites, Pizza Margherita. Step by step, sauce by sauce, cheese by cheese learn how to take your pizza all the way from yeast to “Yay!” And they’re not done yet, not until they make Fried Dough with Cinnamon and Sugar from yeast to “Yum!”

programme.title: Ciao Italia
programme.desc: Mary Ann teams up with Executive Chef David Crinieri to wow her cooking students by exploring the world of perfect pestos. Chef David leads off with a delicate Watercress Pesto tossed with Fresh Spinach Linguine and topped with an Artichoke Crudo. Mary Ann’s up next with her signature Broccolini and Artichoke Pesto tossed with Fusilli. Want perfect pesto every time? Try either one—or both!

programme.title: Modern Pioneering with Georgia Pellegrini
programme.desc: Georgia heads to South Carolina to meet the many talented artisans that define Lowcountry. From a basket weaver who is keeping the sweetgrass tradition of the Gullah Geechee people alive, to an intercoastal inspired shrimp recipe served in ironware crafted right down the street. Find out why the Lowcountry is defined by its character as much as it is a spot on the map.

programme.title: Modern Pioneering with Georgia Pellegrini
programme.desc: Georgia travels to a festival in the Ozarks where she is teaching a cooking class. We follow her as she prepares and gathers her ingredients — we meet a small town chicken farmer, gather produce at the local farmers market, and set the table at a local textile studio where Georgia learns to dye napkins.

programme.title: Modern Pioneering with Georgia Pellegrini
programme.desc: Join Georgia on an urban foraging expedition and learn from an edible and medicinal plants expert as he and Georgia collect a variety of items during a walk through the neighborhood. From flower studded ice cubes, to honeysuckle tea, to dandelion green salad, Georgia is making the most of what is around her.

programme.title: Modern Pioneering with Georgia Pellegrini
programme.desc: Women from around the country convene in west Texas for a transformative experience in the wild. During the episode, Georgia teaches viewers about the different cuts of venison and the ways to cook them, as well as how to save money by grinding your own meat. The guests and viewers will leave feeling empowered and with a different perspective on what it is to be a human and an omnivore.

programme.title: Modern Pioneering with Georgia Pellegrini
programme.desc: Georgia hosts a dinner party at her home in Austin, Texas, focusing on ingredients that are as locally sourced and homemade as possible. Pulling produce from her own garden, she turns it into pickled vegetables, tomato bread salad, a lemon balm cocktail and more! Georgia even decorates the dinner table entirely with items she has found in nature.

programme.title: Today's Homeowner with Danny Lipford
programme.desc: The type and style of flooring in your house has a big impact on the look and feel of your home. We’re exploring some innovative, DIY friendly flooring options that make installing a new floor in your home a whole lot easier.

programme.title: Today's Homeowner with Danny Lipford
programme.desc: These five home improvement projects can really update the look of your home without breaking your budget. Some are do-it-yourself projects, while others are best tackled by a professional.

programme.title: Today's Homeowner with Danny Lipford
programme.desc: Often the most effective enhancements for a home’s interior are the easiest to accomplish. With a few pieces of molding and a little elbow grease we’re making some great improvements for some happy homeowners.

programme.title: Today's Homeowner with Danny Lipford
programme.desc: Buying a house is always a chore; but when it’s your first time, it’s both a challenge and an adventure. And when the house is a fixer-upper, that adventure becomes a way of life. Find out about buying a house in the first of our six-part series.

programme.title: Today's Homeowner with Danny Lipford
programme.desc: In the second of our six-part series, the paperwork is done, but the fun has just begun as our First Time Homeowner tackles much needed renovations before she can move into her new home. Whether it’s repairs or improvements, there’s a lot to learn!

programme.title: Today's Homeowner with Danny Lipford
programme.desc: In the third of our six-part series, we increase the curb appeal on the outside of our First Time Homeowner house by adding a new front porch and replacing the deck, as well as installing new windows, doors, roofing, locks, and siding.

programme.title: Today's Homeowner with Danny Lipford
programme.desc: In the fourth of our six-part First Time Homeowner series, we’re tackling the bathroom. Find out how we made the most of this small space with some big improvements, including a new vanity, countertop, faucets, flooring, and more!

programme.title: Today's Homeowner with Danny Lipford
programme.desc: In the fifth of our six-part First Time Homeowner series, we’re tackling the kitchen. Find out how we made the most of this small space by removing a wall, replacing the cabinets and countertops, tiling the floor, and more!

programme.title: Today's Homeowner with Danny Lipford
programme.desc: In the final installment of our six-part First Time Homeowner series, we tackle the yard and deck. Find out how we landscaped the yard to add curb appeal to the house, put in a crushed limestone driveway, and added a deck.

programme.title: Alternative Dwellings
programme.desc: Sarah and her cat Bodhi have lived in a van for the past two years in western Canada. She gives us a full tour of her classic camper van. Sarah also shares her personal experience about what Vanlife is like for her and why she chose the van that she did.

programme.title: Alternative Dwellings
programme.desc: Electrician builds out a 4x4 sprinter into a modern camper that they are able to both live in and work mobile. This build is done with aluminum and pvc boards, has solar, a composting toilet and everything they need to live comfortably. They also share insights from life on the road.

programme.title: Alternative Dwellings
programme.desc: Virginia lives in her van full-time. She has been traveling North America in her Ford Transit that she designed herself and helped mostly DIY'ers build. Virginia wasn't sure what she was going to do with her retirement until she learned about van life and decided that designing and building out a van to travel and live in was her next creative project.

programme.title: Alternative Dwellings
programme.desc: John worked with professional van builders to create his high tech sprinter van conversion, equipped with a tiltable solar array, lithium batteries and everything he needs to be a digital nomad and travel in northern Canada. He gives us a tour through his tiny home on wheels and shares what Vanlife means to him.

programme.title: Alternative Dwellings
programme.desc: Amazing DIY camper build with beautiful interior woodwork. This 1988 GMC Vandura went from being an old camper to a tiny house that feels nice and cozy inside like a cabin on wheels. This couple has made this small space their home for over two years.

programme.title: Alternative Dwellings
programme.desc: Storm lives full-time in his DIY Astro Minivan while attending university full-time in one of Canada's most expensive cities. He believes this is a good option for students to save money by not having to pay for rent and using the facilities at school to make van living easier.

programme.title: Alternative Dwellings
programme.desc: Meg and Molly, along with their two cats and one dog, live full-time in a van tiny house that they built from a Montreal fire department utility truck. They have been traveling North America for the past two years. Meg built the van out herself with some help for the solar and plumbing.

programme.title: Jail
programme.desc: A woman booked for domestic violence begins screaming and can’t calm down. Two friends arrested for public intoxication embrace in a tearful reunion. Later, a woman is brought in with a stolen car.

programme.title: Jail
programme.desc: After refusing a breathalyzer test, an irate man bangs his head against plexiglass. A man transforms into a “cell warrior” within his holding pen. Soon to be released, an inmate considers the future.

programme.title: Jail
programme.desc: Deputies assist arresting officers by removing an angry man from their patrol car. Violent outbursts plague an arrest. One woman struggles with bail. Three drunk men become wildly emotional.

programme.title: Jail
programme.desc: An angry inmate refuses to change clothes. A man in violation of his parole is stopped by officers for attempting to hitchhike. Two men claim they were beaten by their girlfriends.

programme.title: Jail
programme.desc: In Portland, OR an intoxicated male suspect is brought in for DUI. In Salt Lake City, UT a happy drunk woman is pleasantly cooperative. An emotional detainee sheds some tears while in isolation. In Las Vegas, NV a man is strapped in a restraining chair.

programme.title: Jail
programme.desc: An inmate is subdued for refusing to follow commands. A woman accused of running out on a dinner bill has her jewelry sawed off in jail. After issuing criminal terrorist threats, a man is jailed.

programme.title: Jail
programme.desc: One man booked for intoxication is placed in a sobering cell for being uncooperative. An aggrieved woman claims Animal Control took her “baby.” A man tells officers he is a “child of God” and actor.

programme.title: Jail
programme.desc: Officers warn a man that dressing up like a clown in Texas is not a good idea. A shotgun is found in the hoodie of a man arrested for riding his bicycle without a headlight. A young couple wonders how pot ended up in their possession. Officers are surprised at the amazing amount of pot one can smoke.

programme.title: Jail
programme.desc: In Salt Lake City, UT a belligerent woman is restrained. In Portland, OR a repeat offender is placed in isolation for unruly behavior. In Las Vegas, NV a man opens up about how his friends and family feel about him in a holding cell. In Spokane, WA a man is jailed in an attempt to steal coffee for his friends.

programme.title: Jail
programme.desc: A woman comes clean to having drugs hidden where they can’t be seen. An inmate’s exotic makeup is cleverly created with candy and toothpaste. programme.title: Jail
programme.desc: To avoid self-harm, an intoxicated man is handcuffed to a booking bench. After fighting at a bar, a combative prisoner is arrested. Also, an unmarried couple squabbles during the booking process.

programme.title: Jail
programme.desc: A combative man is placed in a side cell. A transgender woman shares her story. A wild driver is placed in custody, and an intoxicated man is arrested for assault.

programme.title: Jail

programme.title: Jail

programme.title: Baby Einstein Classics
programme.desc: As their communication skills continue to develop, little ones begin to recognize common words and may point to objects or pictures when these items are named. Baby Wordsworth playfully taps into your little one's natural curiosity and introduces 30 words from around the home -- both spoken and in sign language. This engaging program presents little ones with familiar objects in the kitchen, playroom, yard and more -- from the living room window to their favorite teddy bear.

programme.title: Baby Einstein Ocean Explorers
programme.desc: Cal, Neptune, Opus, Dean, Tinker and others learn about the flora and fauna of the ocean through STEAM-based principles and real-life footage.

programme.title: Cal's Sound Yard
programme.desc: Cal's Sound Yard is a new animated series from Baby Einstein that introduces the letters of the alphabet and the sounds they make. Why all the tracing and repetition? And why are the letters out of order? Don't worry! There's a reason for all that; the content is based on "The Einstein Way" - a curiosity-driven learning philosophy, from industry experts, inspired by Montessori and STEAM.

programme.title: Baby Einstein Classics
programme.desc: From the moment babies are born, they use their developing senses to discover the world. In terms of sight, bold patterns in contrasting colors or black and white are initially preferred. As for sounds, Mother s gentle voice, in any language, is music to baby's ears. Language Nursery: Voices From Many Lands acquaints your baby with the sounds of foreign language by presenting delightful, visually stimulating images accompanied by spoken passages, natural sounds and music.

programme.title: Baby Einstein Classics
programme.desc: As each day passes, babies' awareness of the world around them -- and all the wonders it holds -- continues to develop. Wild animals are especially intriguing and World Animals captures the spirtit of little ones' fascination with wild creatures as it introduces them to animals living in the jungle, in the ocean, and on the savannah.

programme.title: Baby Einstein Classics
programme.desc: Set off on a fun-filled "field trip" with your baby to discover animals found at the park, on the farm, and in your own backyard.

programme.title: Baby Einstein Classics
programme.desc: Dive in with your little explorer across the seas to meet amazing animals living in the ocean, coral reef, and polar regions. Overflowing with underwater footage of whales, dolphins, tropical fish, and more, Neptune's Oceans invites you and your baby on an oceanic voyage of discovery!

programme.title: Baby Einstein Classics
programme.desc: Babies love animals, and now you can bring animals from the seven continents home to your baby. Meet dozens of exotic animals including Asian pandas, Australian kangaroos and Arctic snow monkeys...all filmed in their beautiful, natural habitats. Rhythms and songs inspired by faraway lands have been orchestrated especially for little ears and encourage your baby to sing, dance and play to the world beats. You'll laugh together at the funny puppet shows featuring moms and their baby animals.

programme.title: Baby Einstein New Classics
programme.desc: Get moving with Baby Einstein! Real, live-action footage and classical melodies engage young viewers as they discover simple words related to movement. Enjoy encouraging melodies and live-action footage of babies and animals as you learn and move alongside your favorite Baby Einstein friends. Dance along as you watch real, live action footage of animals, children, and toys.

programme.title: Baby Einstein New Classics
programme.desc: Learn about the numbers 1,2,3,4 & 5 through rhyme, real-life footage, counting, stories, and repetition - accompanied by classical music and real world imagery.

programme.title: Baby Einstein Classics
programme.desc: Baby's innate curiosity about all they see and hear increases on a daily basis. Before long, they begin to associate words with objects and to imitate and articulate sounds. Why not give their eyes and ears something wonderful to feast upon? Baby Shakespeare presents little ones with 12 common words that can be found within the rich context of classic poems and the beauty of nature.

programme.title: Baby Einstein Classics
programme.desc: Baby Lullaby takes your baby on a soothing journey with classical lullabies by Bach, Brahms and Mozart. Enjoy relaxing ocean waves, sweet puppet shows and tranquil images. From fun time to rest time, Baby Lullaby is the perfect way to spend special time with your baby.

programme.title: Baby Einstein New Classics
programme.desc: Learn all about music and rhythm in the world around us! Discover classical music and learn instrument names through narration and repetition. Inspire sweet dreams with gentle melodies and a comforting bedtime story. Learn about classical music from Beethoven, Bach, and Mozart. Sing and dance along to classic rhythmic songs like The Wheels on the Bus and see real-life images of instruments.

programme.title: Baby Einstein Classics
programme.desc: At 12 months, babies can identify specific sounds, and they re also starting to match the sounds they hear with the objects that make them. Meet the Orchestra -- First Instruments presents your little "maestro" with a playful introduction to the sights and sounds of a real orchestra -- from the clashing of cymbals to the purring of the cello and everything in between!

programme.title: Wheels on the Bus Nursery Rhymes For Children - Little Treehouse
programme.desc: Wheels on the bus is a nursery rhymes that little babies love! Come join Little Treehouse as they sing, dance in this fun kids educational video with our version of the classic wheels on the bus called the wheels on the bus and many more preschool rhymes for babies like rain rain go away, row row your boat, head shoulder knees and toes and many more

programme.title: Top 10 Nursery Rhymes - Kids TV
programme.desc: Kids songs and nursery rhymes that your little one will love! Come join us, as they sing along, dance in this fun kids videos. Learn preschool songs like This is the way, Old Macdonald, Humpty Dumpty, Five little babies, Twinkle Twinkle, Wheels on the Bus, Finger Family and more.

programme.title: Wheels on the Bus & More Nursery Rhymes - Little Treehouse
programme.desc: Welcome to the mystical and of the Little Treehouse! where you are take non magical nursery rhymes journey. Kids will laugh, dance, sing, and play along with our video, learning kids songs like I spy song, to market to market, animals sounds song, mary had a little lamb, row row row your boat and much, much more while enjoying our friendly characters.

programme.title: Road Rangers Car Cartoons
programme.desc: Enjoy Road Rangers popular family friendly kids stories & adventure Nursery Rhymes for babies and toddlers. Your children will learn essential lessons like problem solving, working together, family bonds along with numbers, colors, shapes, abc and much more in this popular tv kids adventure series.

programme.title: Wheels On The Garbage Truck + More Nursery Rhymes - Little Treehouse
programme.desc: Hey Kids, Little Treehouse is back with more nursery rhymes for toddlers that you will enjoy. Little Treehouse has some amazing animated learning videos and more baby rhymes collection like,Who Stole My Toy, Happy Elephant, Chew Your Food, Doctor Checkup Song, Swimming Song, Peek A Boo, Rock a Bye Baby and many more Kindergarten Songs.

programme.title: Farmees Movie Compilation
programme.desc: Farmees brings you the best of nursery rhymes and kids songs. Your kids are sure to enjoy!

programme.title: Dinobees Funny Cartoon Show for Kids

programme.desc: Momma escapes again from the Mobius Home for Really Bizarre People, disowns Robotnik for failing to capture Sonic, then with the help of an obscure Mobius law, adopts Sonic!

programme.desc: When S and G rocket to outerspace to mine a radioactive mineral from asteroids, Sonic and Tails stow away. Through a blunder by the robots, all are stranded in space, and Robotnik tries to blow all of them out of the stars.

programme.title: Sonic the Hedgehog, Adventures of
programme.desc: When a Rebel Base is threatened by Robotnik, Sonic and Tails team up with Big Mike and Da Bears to run a booby-trapped gauntlet in their gigantic Road Warrior-like truck.

programme.title: Sonic the Hedgehog, Adventures of
programme.desc: Robotnik pretends to retire, and lures Sonic and Tails into a phony vacation resort, laden with booby-traps set by Scratch and Grounder. But Sonic takes the bungee jumping, hang gliding, and skydiving in stride, and gives the bashes the bots.

programme.title: Sonic the Hedgehog, Adventures of
programme.desc: Brandon Quark, an evil half-man, half-duck genius challenges, then teams up with Robotnik to capture Sonic. But Sonic knows their weakness, and pits the Badnik geniuses against one another, and sets himself free.

programme.title: Sonic the Hedgehog, Adventures of
programme.desc: When Wes Weasley returns to the Fortress, Coconuts tricks Robotnik into signing an IOU so he can buy thousands of Mobiums worth of magic tricks to use on Sonic. In the end, Sonic goes free, and Robotnik is forced to pay Wes the massive IOU debt.

programme.title: Sonic the Hedgehog, Adventures of
programme.desc: Sonic plays Cyrano to bring Robotnik Jr. and Breezie together. The problem is, Breezie's crazy about Sonic. So Sonic gets out of the way, but when he does, Junior is captured by Robotnik, and Breezie comes to the rescue, finally falling for him.

programme.title: Sonic the Hedgehog
programme.desc: Sonic attempts a solo rescue to prevent prisoners from being roboti-sized and is hit by a Memory Scrambler that erases short-term memory.

programme.title: Rainbow Ruby
programme.desc: A welcome party for Prince Frederick is taking place in Rainbow Village, but the guest of honour isn't having any fun at all. Rainbow Ruby uses her magic suitcase to become a party planner. // The village is a total mess from the paint that spilled from up above. Rainbow Ruby uses her magic suitcase to turn into a clean-up engineer and starts cleaning with her friends.

programme.title: Rainbow Ruby
programme.desc: A Flower Festival is underway in Rainbow Village and Ling Ling has called Rainbow Ruby to help her judge the "best flower" contest. But just as Rainbow Ruby starts looking around at the flowers, Kiki's pet dog Pom Pom starts acting unusual and running around frantically, destroying the festival! // Gina and Jessy are with a huge fish that's injured by the lake. Rainbow Ruby becomes a Marine Biologist. Rainbow Ruby uses her encyclopedia to find out that the fish is actually a dolphin.

programme.title: Rainbow Ruby
programme.desc: Everyone is frightened because some visiting birds keep trying to sit on their heads. According to Chirpee, the birds just enjoy sitting on people's heads. programme.title: Rainbow Ruby
programme.desc: Gina accidentally broke Felicia's house, leaving Felicia homeless. She needs strawberries for the Rainbow Fruit Ice Cream Festival that's starting soon but the strawberries are all mixed in with other red objects.

programme.title: Rainbow Ruby
programme.desc: Thunderbell is sad because he's discovered a new fruit and eaten all of it. Now there's none left and he'll never be able to eat that fruit again. programme.title: Rainbow Ruby
programme.desc: Ling Ling takes Rainbow Ruby to Ellie's cabin where she meets mischievous baby stars that fell from the sky. programme.title: Rainbow Ruby

programme.title: Rainbow Ruby

programme.title: Rainbow Ruby
